Japanese: １２月３０日　午後　２時１７分 地方裁判所　被告人第５控え室
English: December 30, 2:17 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 5

Japanese: まさか‥‥あの、アクロが！
English: I can't believe it!! Acro!?

Japanese: やっぱり、ショックですか‥‥？
English: It's pretty shocking, isn't it?

Japanese: そりゃそうだあ。アイツ、 いちばんマトモだったのに‥‥
English: It definitely is! And to think he was always the most straight-forward of the group.

Japanese: オラ‥‥ここまで キラわれたんだあ！
English: Geebus... Unbelievable...

Japanese: あー、おほん。
English: *Ahem!*

Japanese: でも！　アクロさん、ワザと 罪を着せようとしたワケじゃ‥‥
English: Acro tried to pin the murder on you on purpose...

Japanese: うう‥‥そうだんべか？
English: He... He did?

Japanese: ゴホッゴホン！　‥‥げふっ！
English: *Psst!* *Psst!* *Cough!*

Japanese: オラ‥‥ただの キラワレ者なんだあ‥‥。
English: My my... He's just a little twerp, isn't he...?

Japanese: そんなことないですから。
English: Well... that's one way to put it.

Japanese: コラァ！　アンタたちッ！
English: *Cough!* Hey! Hey pal!!

Japanese: ヒトがセッカク、証拠品を 持ってきたのに‥‥ムシッスかッ！
English: You're gonna ignore me after I went to all this trouble to bring you some evidence!?

Japanese: あ。イトノコ刑事さん‥‥。
English: Ahh... Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: もう、いッス。帰るッス。 有罪でもカッ食らえばいいッス。
English: Ahh, forget it. I'm goin' home. This guy deserves to be guilty anyways...

Japanese: まま、まあまあ。 ゆっくりしてってくださいよ。
English: Now now Detective. I'm sorry. Now, why don't you relax a little.

Japanese: おいしい牛乳もありますから。
English: We've got some really tasty milk!

Japanese: 楽しいトランプの手品は どうだい？　ミスター。
English: How about a card trick, Detective?

Japanese: はっはっはっ。 いやー、しかたないッスねー。
English: Ho ho ho... Well, if you insist...

Japanese: で？　で？　“証拠品” って‥‥なんですか？
English: Now about that evidence you mentioned... What is it!?

Japanese: これッス。
English: Here ya go.

Japanese: あれ。‥‥これ、きのう アクロさんの部屋で‥‥？
English: Huh? This was... Yesterday in Acro's room...

Japanese: 分析結果が出たッス。 ‥‥あとで見ておくといいッス。
English: The result of our investigation... You can look at it later.

Japanese: こんなコトしちゃって、 かるま検事に怒られませんか？
English: Won't Von Karma be mad that you're doing this?

Japanese: ないしょッス。
English: That's why this is all a secret.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ベツにワレワレは、アンタに 味方するワケじゃないッス。
English: Look, details are on a need to know basis. And we're not really allies or anything...

Japanese: ここまでの法廷の流れは、 ワレワレの予想どおりッス。
English: But everything that's happened in court up until now has gone according to our plan.

Japanese: かるま検事、かなりアセってた みたいだけど‥‥。
English: Von Karma looked like she was in a pretty big hurry though.

Japanese: ‥‥いずれわかるッス。
English: You'll figure it out eventually, pal...

Japanese: ワレワレはきのうのうちに、 最後の手を打っておいたッス。
English: Yesterday, our final plans were put into place.

Japanese: “最後の手”‥‥？
English: "Final plans"...?

Japanese: あー。それから、検事から 伝言があるッス。
English: Uh huh. That reminds me... I've got a message from the prosecutor for you.

Japanese: “判決のゆくえは、 最後の瞬間に決まる”
English: "Judgment comes at the very last instant."

Japanese: ‥‥以上ッス。 じゃ。自分は帰るッス。
English: That's it. And that's it for me too, pal. I'm goin' home.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？ “最後の瞬間”って‥‥。
English: What did he mean by that... "The very last instant" part.

Japanese: さ、さあ‥‥
English: Hmm... Everything he said seemed pretty cryptic to me...

Japanese: あ。そういえば。
English: Oh... One more thing.

Japanese: きゃっ。
English: Ahhh! Don't scare me like that!

Japanese: 被告に、サーカスの芸人たちから 大量の差し入れが来てるッス。
English: It looks like there's a large care package from the circus for the defendant.

Japanese: え。オラに‥‥？
English: Eh? For me...?

Japanese: 牛乳ッス。
English: It's milk.

Japanese: ‥‥受付が、給食センター みたいになってるッスから、
English: The reception area looks like some kind of dairy,

Japanese: 早く飲むッスよ。
English: so hurry up and drink it before it spoils.

Japanese: みんなが‥‥オラに‥‥？
English: An entire dairy's worth of milk... For me!?

Japanese: 同日　午後２時２７分 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: December 30, 2:27 PM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: では、審理を再開しましょう。
English: This court is now in session. Shall we continue our proceedings?

Japanese: 狩魔検事。先ほどの つづきから、おねがいします。
English: Ms. von Karma. Please continue from where you left off.

Japanese: アクロ氏に証言をおねがいする ‥‥被害者との関係について。
English: I'd like to continue with Acro's testimony, starting with his relationship to the victim.

Japanese: その上で、弁護側に 立証してもらうわ。
English: I'd also like to get proof from the defense...

Japanese: ‥‥この証人に、いったい どんな動機があるのか‥‥をね。
English: Proof of what kind of motive Acro would have to commit this crime.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 では、証人。
English: Understood. Now Mr. Dingling.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 証言をおねがいします。
English: Please proceed with your testimony.

Japanese: ついに“ドーキ”だよ。 だいじょうぶ？　なるほどくん。
English: Finally we get to the motive! Wait... Nick, are you OK?

Japanese: ‥‥ヘンジに困る質問を しないでほしいな。
English: Just do me a favor and don't ask questions you don't want the answer to...
*gulp*

Japanese: ～被害者・立見団長について～
English: -- About the Ringmaster --

Japanese: 小さいころ、オレたち 両親に捨てられたんです。
English: When we were little, we were abandoned by our parents.

Japanese: それで、立見団長のサーカスに 引き取られることに。
English: That's when the Ringmaster of the Berry Big Circus, Russell Berry, took us in.

Japanese: アクロバットは、 ９才のころからやっています。
English: I became an acrobat at around nine years old.

Japanese: ‥‥団長に、恩返しがしたかった。 そのために生きていたのに‥‥。
English: I wanted to find a way to repay the Ringmaster. That was my sole purpose in life...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ 感心なワカモノですなあ。
English: Hmm... You're such a thoughtful young man.

Japanese: お聞きのとおり、証人は被害者に 大きな恩を感じている。
English: As you heard, the witness deeply respected the victim.

Japanese: こんなアクロ氏が、どうして 団長を殺害できたと言うのかしら？
English: I wonder how anyone could think that Acro could kill the man he held in such esteem.

Japanese: まったく、そのとおりですね‥‥。 いかがですかな、弁護人。
English: You are absolutely right... How could anyone think that, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 尋問するまでもないと 思いますが‥‥？
English: Which is why there's no real need for a cross-examination is there?

Japanese: （ぼくが今、 求められているのは‥‥）
English: (Actually, that's the question I'm trying to answer myself...)

Japanese: （“アクロさんがなぜ、 団長を殺害したか？”‥‥）
English: ("Why would Acro kill the Ringmaster?"...)

Japanese: （まだわかってないのなら、 これが最後のチャンス、か）
English: (This might be my last chance to answer that question.)

Japanese: 尋問は、弁護側のケンリです！
English: The defense has a right to cross-examine the witness!

Japanese: フッ。見苦しいわね ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Hmph. You're so tactless, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: なりふりかまわず、なんとか 動機をデッチ上げるつもり‥‥？
English: You don't care about justice, do you? You just want to fabricate a motive...

Japanese: よろしい！ では、尋問をおねがいします。
English: Very well, Mr. Wright. Cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: ～被害者・立見団長について～
English: -- About the Ringmaster --

Japanese: 小さいころ、オレたち 両親に捨てられたんです。
English: When we were little, we were abandoned by our parents.

Japanese: それで、立見団長のサーカスに 引き取られることに。
English: That's when the Ringmaster of the Berry Big Circus, Russell Berry, took us in.

Japanese: アクロバットは、 ９才のころからやっています。
English: I became an acrobat at around nine years old.

Japanese: ‥‥団長に、恩返しがしたかった。 そのために生きていたのに‥‥。
English: I wanted to find a way to repay the Ringmaster. That was my sole purpose in life...

Japanese: ‥‥どうかな？　なるほどくん。
English: What do you think, Nick?

Japanese: なんか‥‥尋問すればするほど、 心証が悪くなる気がする。
English: I dunno... I think the more I cross-examine him, the worse I end up looking in the end.

Japanese: そ、それって‥‥。
English: Y-You mean...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥この尋問、 ワナ‥‥だったみたいだよ。
English: I get the feeling that this cross-examination was a trap.

Japanese: うう‥‥また、 かるま検事が‥‥。
English: Yeah... Von Karma set you up again...

Japanese: “オレたち”‥‥？
English: "We"?

Japanese: オレと、弟の一平です。
English: Yes. My brother Sean and I.

Japanese: ほお‥‥弟さんが‥‥？
English: What? You have a brother?

Japanese: ‥‥それは、いくつのときの ことだったの？　証人。
English: How old were you when this happened, Acro?

Japanese: オレが８才で‥‥弟は４才 でしたね。
English: I was eight years old and my brother was four.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ ヒドいご両親ですなあ。
English: Hmm... Your parents were very cruel, weren't they?

Japanese: 今はオレたち、親なんか ウラんじゃいませんよ。
English: Nowadays, we aren't bitter about what happened to us.

Japanese: ‥‥タチミ・サーカスの仲間に 出会えたわけですからね。
English: Because it allowed us to meet the wonderful people at the Berry Big Circus.

Japanese: 裁判長、目をうるませてるよ。
English: Nick, the judge is getting misty eyed.

Japanese: （こういうハナシには ヨワいみたいだ‥‥）
English: (He's got a soft spot for sob stories it looks like...)

Japanese: もぎッ！
English: OWWW!! *sniff* *sniff*

Japanese: 泣いてないで、さっさと進める。
English: There's no crying in court! Let's keep going!

Japanese: うう‥‥証人。 証言をつづけてください‥‥。
English: *sniff* The witness may proceed with his testimony...

Japanese: 団長とあなたは、 どういうカンケイに‥‥？
English: How would you describe your relationship with the Ringmaster?

Japanese: オジにあたりますね。 オレのオヤジの兄です。
English: He was like an uncle, a father, and a big brother all rolled up into one.

Japanese: ‥‥他に、たよれる 親せきもいなくて‥‥。
English: The Ringmaster and my brother were the only family I had...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥サーカスですか‥‥
English: Hmm... What about other people at the circus?

Japanese: 今から１５年以上も前のことです。
English: This was over 15 years ago...

Japanese: 当時は、ゼンゼンお客さんが 入らなかったものですから、
English: Back then, there were very few customers coming in,

Japanese: オレたちのメンドウなんて 見るヨユウは、なかったんです。
English: so no one really had the time to look after us. They were worried about other things.

Japanese: でも‥‥団長は、いつも オレたちの前では笑っていました。
English: But the Ringmaster... He would always come see us with a laugh and a smile.

Japanese: ‥‥うつくしいお話ね。
English: What a beautiful story...

Japanese: 団長のために、何かできないか‥‥ オレ、いつも考えてました。
English: That's why I was always thinking of what I could do to help. I wanted to thank him.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥イイ人だよ、アクロさん。
English: Nick! Isn't Acro such a wonderful person!?

Japanese: それはわかってるよ。 （だから、ツラいんだ）
English: I know. He seems like a nice guy... (Which is what makes this so difficult.)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥それで、 あなたはいつから、曲芸を？
English: Hmm... So then how long have you been a performer?

Japanese: そんな小さい子供が アクロバットを‥‥？
English: You started off as an acrobat at that early of an age?

Japanese: 団長にムリを言って やらせてもらいました。
English: I begged the Ringmaster until he finally agreed to let me do it.

Japanese: オレ、そのころから からだがデカかったから。
English: Ever since then, I've been in incredible physical shape.

Japanese: そのうち、弟とコンビを 組むようになりました。
English: That's also when I decided to form a group with my brother.

Japanese: タチミ・フライング・チーム といえば、ちょっと有名ですよ。
English: We called ourselves the Flying Dingling-Berrys. It's nearly a household name.

Japanese: ‥‥私もアメリカで 聞いたことがあるわ。
English: I've even heard of them in Germany...

Japanese: （ウソつけ！）
English: (Liar!)

Japanese: とにかくオレ、何か役に 立ちたかったんですよ。
English: The point is that I wanted to be of some use to the circus.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥ ますます感心なワカモノですねえ。
English: Hmm... You are truly a remarkable young man.

Japanese: （裁判長の目が、どんどん やさしくなっていくな‥‥）
English: (The judge keeps looking at Acro almost like a proud father...)
Hmph...

Japanese: ‥‥団長と、何かトラブルは なかったんですか？
English: Did you ever have any trouble with the Ringmaster?

Japanese: いてッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: なんてコトを言うの！ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: How could you ask such a thing, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: この証言を聞いても、 あなたにはわからないのかしら？
English: Do you have some sort of fundamental misunderstanding of this witness' testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥証人のヤサシイキモチが。
English: Or the heart-felt emotions contained within?

Japanese: 思い知りなさいッ！ 成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: You better think about this, Mr. Phoenix Wright! You better think hard!

Japanese: いてッ！ あたたたッ！
English: OWWW! OW-OW-OW-OWWW!!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ やはり、どう考えても、
English: Hmm... No matter how you look at it,

Japanese: この証人が被害者のイノチを ねらうなど、考えられませんね。
English: there's no way I could see this witness ever taking the victim's life.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。
English: Exactly.

Japanese: そのコトバが聞きたかったわ ‥‥裁判長。
English: I've been waiting for you to say that, Your Honor.

Japanese: なるほどくん、 あたしもそう思うよ。
English: Nick, I hate to say it, but I agree with them.

Japanese: 追いつめてるぼくが、 なんだかワルモノみたいだな‥‥。
English: I was trying to chase down the truth, but I ended up just looking like a jerk...

Japanese: ‥‥尋問はしません。
English: There's no need to cross- examine this witness.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: W-What's that!?

Japanese: “どうして立見団長が 殺害されることになったのか？”
English: "Why was the Ringmaster murdered?"

Japanese: ‥‥そのコタエを知るのに、 アクロさんの尋問は必要ない！
English: There is no need to delve into that bit of testimony when I know the answer already!

Japanese: ‥‥いや、 もう、じゅうぶんでしょう。
English: I think that will be enough for now.

Japanese: この証人が被害者の殺害を 考えるなど‥‥
English: Pondering whether or not this witness would kill the Ringmaster leads me to

Japanese: あり得ないようですね。
English: believe that is pretty much unlikely.

Japanese: そのとおり‥‥ね。
English: Exactly right, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 あなたにお聞きしたい。
English: Mr. Wright... I'd like to ask you a question.

Japanese: はい。
English: Go ahead, Your Honor.

Japanese: どうですか？　あなたに 証明できるのですか？
English: What was Acro's motive for killing the Ringmaster?

Japanese: アクロ氏が立見団長を殺害した ‥‥その動機を！
English: How about it? Can you explain that to me?

Japanese: ‥‥ここまできて“できない” とは言えません。
English: Now that I've come this far, there is no way I could answer "No, I can't..."

Japanese: それは、たのもしいわね‥‥
English: Oh... This is gonna be good...

Japanese: ‥‥では、提示して いただきましょう。
English: Very well, then please enlighten the court.

Japanese: アクロ氏が立見　七百人を 殺害した動機を示す証拠を！
English: Let's see some evidence that proves Acro's motive for killing the Ringmaster!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。カクニンします。
English: Umm... Let me just go over this one more time...

Japanese: アクロさんが、立見団長を 殺害した動機ですよ‥‥？
English: This is why Acro killed the Ringmaster?

Japanese: ‥‥そうですよね。
English: That is correct.

Japanese: 弁護人も、カクニンを おねがいできるかしら。
English: I don't think the defense would mind confirming something for me.

Japanese: そんな証拠で、いったい 何をねらっているのか‥‥？
English: What exactly are we supposed to learn from this piece of evidence?

Japanese: ‥‥何をねらってる、って‥‥
English: What are you supposed to learn... Hmm...

Japanese: 今年のＮＧ大賞、かしら？
English: Looks like you just won the award for Biggest Failure In Court.

Japanese: ‥‥えぬじー、だって。
English: Ouch... Biggest Failure In Court... That stings!

Japanese: ＮＧ大賞の賞品を与えます！
English: In recognition of your honor, let me bestow you with my special prize!

Japanese: ‥‥さあ、どうしますか弁護人。 早く、立証をおねがいします！
English: So now what will you do, Mr. Wright? I would prefer you hurry up and prove something!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥ここは、 よく考えたほうがいいぞ！）
English: (Drats! Now I'm really gonna have to think about this...)

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥今度こそ、当てて見せます！
English: OK... This time, I won't leave any doubt in your mind!

Japanese: 言っておきますが、私は射的屋の オヤジじゃありませんぞ！
English: May the defense remember that this court is not target practice for its theories!

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥？
English: Nick...?

Japanese: （‥‥そう、考えるまでもない。 最初から、明白なことだった！）
English: (Yeah... I didn't even have to think about it... It was obvious from the start!)

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 団長を殺害した動機‥‥ それを立証するのは不可能です。
English: The reason that Acro killed the Ringmaster is something that can't be proven.

Japanese: な、なんですと！
English: W-What!?

Japanese: だって、アクロさんが被害者を 殺害する理由はありませんからね。
English: That's because Acro had no reason to kill the Ringmaster at all.

Japanese: あいて！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: バ‥‥バカのバカさカゲンは バカにできないものね‥‥
English: Your foolish attempts to fool us like foolish fools is so fool-heartedly foolish!

Japanese: 忘れたの！　あなたは、 この証人を告発したのよ？
English: Did you forget!? You made an accusation against this witness, did you not?

Japanese: “立見　七百人殺害の真犯人” ‥‥としてね！
English: I believe it was... "This is the real killer of Russell Berry, Ringmaster."

Japanese: 結果的には‥‥ そういうことになります。
English: If you want to jump to the end of things... Then, yes, that sounds about right.

Japanese: け、結果的‥‥？
English: The end of things...?

Japanese: アクロさん。
English: Acro.

Japanese: もちろん、あなたは団長を 殺害するつもりなどなかった。
English: You didn't plan to kill the Ringmaster at all, did you?

Japanese: あなたの本当のねらいは、 団長ではなかったのですからね。
English: The Ringmaster wasn't your target that night.

Japanese: な‥‥何を言いだすの‥‥！
English: What did you just say!?

Japanese: アクロさんがこの犯行で 亡きものにしたかった人物は、
English: I'm saying that the target of this witness' murder plot was not the Ringmaster.

Japanese: 立見　七百人ではなかったのです！
English: He did not plan to kill Russell Berry!

Japanese: ええええッ！
English: W-WHAAATT?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 係官、かまいませんから、 うるさい人はつまみ出しなさい！
English: Bailiff, I don't care who it is, smack anyone who's loud in the face! Twice if you must!

Japanese: 弁護人！ ‥‥あ、あなたはいったい‥‥
English: Mr. Wright! What in the world are you trying to do to my court!?

Japanese: あひゃは！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一！ ‥‥あ、あなたはいったい‥‥
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright! What in the world are you trying to do to his court!?

Japanese: いったい、だれだったと言うのッ！ アクロ氏が殺害しようとしたのは！
English: Are you attempting to imply that Acro was trying to kill someone else!?

Japanese: これがッ！ しょうこッ！
English: This is OWWW! My evidence!! OWWW!

Japanese: ‥‥なな、ナニするんだッ！ いててッ
English: W-What are you doing!? OWWW!!
There's fourteen more where that came from!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってください！
English: H-Hold it right there!

Japanese: それはまた、あとでゆっくり やっていただくとして‥‥、
English: You can take your time with the other fourteen later...

Japanese: 今の証拠、もう一度 見せてください。
English: But now, let me see that evidence once again.

Japanese: （ダメだ。 コワくて出せない‥‥！）
English: (Erk! I'm so scared of what will happen, I can't even move to give him the evidence...)

Japanese: マジメに答えなさい！ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: He asked you for something!! Where are your manners, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: ‥‥立見　里香‥‥。
English: Regina Berry...?

Japanese: 団長のムスメにあたる少女 ‥‥ですね？
English: This young girl is the Ringmaster's daughter, correct?

Japanese: アクロさん。
English: Acro.

Japanese: あなたの本当のねらいは 彼女だった‥‥そうですね？
English: You were really aiming for her that night, weren't you?

Japanese: 答える必要はないわ！ ‥‥これは、悪質な誘導尋問よ！
English: You don't need to answer that! It's a mean-spirited leading question!!

Japanese: この質問には、カンタンに 答えられる！
English: He could easily answer this question!

Japanese: ちがうのならば“ちがう” ‥‥それだけのことです！
English: If I'm wrong, all he has to say is, "You're wrong." That's it.

Japanese: それならば！ ‥‥そう、成歩堂　龍一！
English: That's it, huh Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥そこまで！ いいですか、成歩堂くん‥‥
English: Enough! Mr. Wright, allow me to--

Japanese: わはほッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥私の発言を止めるなんて、 １００年早い。
English: The only thing allowed to interrupt me is death itself!

Japanese: は。
English: Huh!?

Japanese: あなたも弁護士なら ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: And that goes for you too... Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: 証拠を見せなさい！
English: Show me evidence! Now!

Japanese: この証人の、立見　里香に対する 殺意を証明する証拠を！
English: I want to know why Acro would want to kill Regina Berry!

Japanese: そ、そう、それです！ この私に提示してください！
English: Y-Y-Yes! Me too! I demand to see some proof!

Japanese: アクロ氏が、その少女に殺意を 抱いていたという証拠を！
English: Present evidence that proves Acro was out to kill this young girl!

Japanese: ‥‥いいですか、弁護人！
English: Alright, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ここまできたら、 証拠のチカラがすべてなのよ！
English: At this point, the only thing that's important is evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥ハッタリだけでは、 私の目は‥‥
English: This better not be a bluff. You're not going to--

Japanese: わっは！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: 私の目はごまかせないわ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: You're not going to fool me... Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: なんか、イキが合ってきたよ あのふたり‥‥
English: Those two are finishing each other's sentences now...

Japanese: ムチでたたき、たたかれるうち、 ナニかが生まれたのかも。
English: It must be the special bond between whipper and whipped.

Japanese: さあ！　私たちに 提示してください！
English: My patience is running thin, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥アクロさん。 コイツに見おぼえがありますね？
English: Acro... Do you have any recollection of seeing this?

Japanese: ‥‥それは‥‥
English: That's...

Japanese: 立見団長の衣装のポケットから 発見されたメモです。
English: It's a piece of paper that we found inside the Ringmaster's tailcoat.

Japanese: 被害者のポケット‥‥？
English: Inside the victim's tailcoat?

Japanese: アクロ氏が書いたものです。 “殺人者に告ぐ”‥‥
English: Acro wrote this note. It's ironically entitled "To The Murderer!"...

Japanese: 事件の夜１０時、広場へ呼び出す ための手紙でした。 10:00 PM.
English: It's purpose was to call someone to the plaza at

Japanese: 立見　七百人は、その手紙で 呼び出されたわけですか‥‥！
English: So you're saying that he called Russell Berry with that note...!?

Japanese: ‥‥ただ、 ここで１つ、問題があります。
English: There's just one little problem...

Japanese: モンダイ‥‥？
English: Problem?

Japanese: アクロ氏は、これをある人物の ポケットに入れた。
English: Acro did indeed place this note into someone's pocket.

Japanese: しかし、それは立見団長では なかった！
English: However, that someone was not the Ringmaster!

Japanese: ま‥‥まさか、それが‥‥？
English: You mean... It wasn't for the...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。
English: That's exactly what I mean.

Japanese: その人物こそ、 ‥‥立見　里香さんだったのです！
English: The person this note was intended for was none other than Regina Berry!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ な、成歩堂くん！　今の話は‥‥
English: Order! Order! Order! M-Mr. Wright! This little theory of yours...

Japanese: 本当のことです。
English: It's the truth, Your Honor. It isn't a theory.

Japanese: ただ、ミリカさんは、その手紙が 自分あてのものだと思わなかった。
English: Simply put, Regina didn't think the note was meant for her.

Japanese: ‥‥だから、それを事件の朝、 食堂に張っておいたのです。
English: Which is why, the morning of the crime, she placed it on the cafeteria bulletin board.

Japanese: それを‥‥父親の団長が 読んでしまったわけ‥‥？
English: That's when her father... I mean the Ringmaster... Read the note?

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: That's correct.

Japanese: 団長は、ミリカさんのかわりに 現場に行ったのです！
English: The Ringmaster ended up in that plaza instead of Regina!

Japanese: そして、殺害された。 ‥‥ミリカさんのかわりに！
English: And he was killed because of that mistake... Instead of Regina!!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そんなことが‥‥
English: That's... That's... That's incredible!

Japanese: さきほどの、アクロさんの 証言を思い出してください！
English: Remember the testimony that Acro gave us earlier today!

Japanese: ‥‥胸像を持って、窓から 身を乗り出すのはムリですね。
English: Lifting the bust and looking out of the window would've been impossible.

Japanese: オレの下半身は、今はチカラを 入れることができないんです。
English: There's no way I could have exerted that kind of force on my lower body.

Japanese: そんなことしたら、 オレのほうが落っこちますよ。
English: If I were to do that, I'd end up falling out the window myself.

Japanese: アクロさんは、窓の下に来たのが “だれ”だったのか‥‥
English: Acro had no idea who it was that arrived in the plaza...

Japanese: 見ることができなかったんです。
English: Because he couldn't look down out of his window to see who it actually was.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥！　アクロ氏は、 ミリカさんがいると思って‥‥
English: I've got it! I've got it! Acro thought it was Regina down in the plaza...

Japanese: 胸像を落としたのです。
English: And that's when he let the bust fly.

Japanese: ‥‥ね。なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick...

Japanese: ミリカちゃん、これを今、 傍聴席で聞いてるんだよね？
English: Isn't Regina listening to all of this from the audience?

Japanese: うん。‥‥これからもっと、 キビしいハナシになるはずだよ。
English: She is. Unfortunately, it's only going to get harsher from here.

Japanese: ‥‥ミリカちゃん‥‥、 しんぼうできるのかな‥‥
English: I hope Regina can handle it...

Japanese: アクロさんが、ミリカさんに このメモを書いた‥‥！
English: Acro wrote this note to Regina...!

Japanese: ‥‥バカはバカなりのバカバカしい バカがバカにバカなバカのバカはバ
English: Foolishly foolish fool with foolishly foolish fool ideas of foolish tomfoolery...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥自分で言ってて よくわからなくなってきた。
English: ...You're so foolish, you've even made me sound like a foolhardy fool...

Japanese: とにかく、成歩堂　龍一！ このメモには、こう書いてあるわ！
English: Very well, Mr. Phoenix Wright. If you're so sure, then tell us about this line!

Japanese: “事件の決定的な証拠を あずかっている”‥‥
English: "I have conclusive evidence of what took place."

Japanese: そうですね。
English: Uh huh. That's a line in the note alright.

Japanese: つまり‥‥
English: Well, then if the note was sent to Regina Berry, it must mean that...

Japanese: 立見　里香を“殺人者” として告発していることになる！
English: This note is declaring that Regina Berry is a murderer!

Japanese: ‥‥よくわかってるじゃないか。
English: You just don't get it, do you?

Japanese: な‥‥なにッ！
English: What!? ...What did you just say!?

Japanese: 団長は、その事件を知っていた！ だからあの夜現場に行ったんです。
English: The Ringmaster knew what the note meant! Which is why he went to the plaza.

Japanese: ‥‥かわいいムスメさんの かわりに、ね！
English: ...In place of his lovely daughter!

Japanese: ま‥‥待ちなさい、成歩堂くん！
English: H-Hold it right there, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あなたは、メモに書かれている “事件”を知っているのですか！
English: What is this incident that is alluded to in the note?

Japanese: （半年前の“事件”‥‥）
English: (The incident six months ago...)

Japanese: ‥‥いやあ、どうでしょう。
English: Urk! Umm... Well...

Japanese: ハナシはカンタンよ‥‥裁判長。
English: This is simple, Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護人が“半年前の事件”を 立証できないということは、
English: If Mr. Phoenix Wright can't provide the facts for this so- called incident on the note,

Japanese: このメモに書かれていることが ジジツかどうかわからない‥‥
English: then there is no way to know whether this note is relevant or not.

Japanese: つまり、立見　里香に対する 殺意も立証できないというコト！
English: Moreover, one cannot prove any sort of motive for wanting to murder Regina Berry!

Japanese: ‥‥え。 そ、そういうコトなんですか？
English: Huh? It's that simple?

Japanese: モチロンそういうコトですね！
English: Of course it's that simple!

Japanese: そういうコトだって！ なるほどくん！
English: Nick! I thought you were going somewhere with this!

Japanese: （マズい！　ここは どんな手を使っても‥‥）
English: (This is getting ugly... All I can do to get out of this mess is...)

Japanese: （“半年前の事件”の真相を 立証しなければ！）
English: (I have to prove the truth about what happened six months ago!)

Japanese: “半年前の事件”‥‥
English: An incident occurred six months ago...

Japanese: 弁護側にはもちろん、 立証の用意があります！
English: And now I am more than ready to show this court what happened at that time!

Japanese: ‥‥‥バカな！
English: ...Moron!

Japanese: しかし！　それは、今回の 事件にカンケイあるのですか？
English: Wait! Are you sure that it relates to the present case!?

Japanese: も、もちろんです！
English: It does indeed, Your Honor!

Japanese: “半年前の事件”‥‥すべては そこから始まっていたのです！
English: Everything in this case has its start in what happened six months ago!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだったの？ なるほどくん！
English: Really, Nick?

Japanese: た、たぶん。
English: I... Umm... I think so.

Japanese: ‥‥それならば、さっそく 聞かせてもらうわ。
English: Well then if that's the case, hurry up and tell us about it.

Japanese: このメモに書かれている “事件の決定的な証拠”‥‥
English: What is this "conclusive evidence" mentioned in the note?

Japanese: これはいったい、 なんのことなの！
English: I know I'd certainly like to know what it is!

Japanese: （これに答えられなければ、 ハッタリが見抜かれる！）
English: (If I can't answer that question, the judge is going to think I'm bluffing!)

Japanese: 半年前の“事件”。メモに書かれた “決定的な証拠”とは‥‥
English: The "conclusive evidence" about the incident six months ago is actually...

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか、これは。
English: What...? This is it?

Japanese: いや、その‥‥
English: Well... Umm...

Japanese: いらない証拠品を 整理しようかと思って。
English: I kinda figured that I'd try and get rid of evidence I didn't need any more.

Japanese: そういうことは、 おうちでやっておきなさい！
English: Well, from now on, you can do those sorts of things at home, on your own time!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: Whoops!

Japanese: 正しい証拠品をおねがいします。
English: Please present this CONCLUSIVE evidence, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （‥‥いよいよ大ヅメが近い‥‥）
English: (I'm almost to the big climax of this case...)

Japanese: （‥‥これ以上まちがえる ワケにはいかない！）
English: (Which means that, no matter what, I can't mess up again!)

Japanese: むむむむ‥‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: はっくしょ！　はっくしょ！ はっくしょはがッ！
English: *Achoo!* *Achoo!* *Achoo!* *Achoo!* *Achoo!* *Achoo!*

Japanese: これは‥‥なんの冗談かしら？ 成歩堂　龍一！
English: What kind of spicy joke is this... Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: 冗談なんかじゃありませんよ。
English: It isn't a joke at all. It's the decisive evidence you asked for.

Japanese: どど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: W-W-What do you mean?

Japanese: 被害者は、木箱を持ち去ろうと していました‥‥。
English: The victim would arrive and try to take away the wooden box.

Japanese: その箱の中に“決定的な証拠”が 入っていたからです！
English: That's when they'd discover the decisive evidence found inside!

Japanese: まま、またトンでもないコトを！
English: Another unbelievable conclusion!

Japanese: じゃあ弁護人！ あなたはこう言うワケですか！
English: Very well, Mr. Wright. So what exactly are you saying?

Japanese: “立見　里香さんは、この小ビンで だれかを殺害した”‥‥と！
English: Are you claiming Regina Berry killed someone with a small bottle of pepper!?

Japanese: そうとしか考えられません！
English: Taking the note into account, that's the only logical conclusion you can draw...

Japanese: ‥‥バカのバカげた バカさわぎというヤツね‥‥
English: ...Foolish fool who never tires of his own foolish ways...

Japanese: それならば、聞かせて ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: If you're so sure, Mr. Phoenix Wright, then answer this question!

Japanese: 立見　里香の手によって ギセイになったのは、だれ？
English: Who was Regina Berry's intended victim?

Japanese: ‥‥だ、ダメだよ！ ヒトを勝手に死なせちゃ！
English: What's wrong with you!! You can't just pick random people!

Japanese: なんですか今のは？
English: What was that just now?

Japanese: す‥‥すみません。 ちょっと、練習をですね‥‥
English: I'm... S-Sorry. I was just... Umm... Practicing.

Japanese: そんなことは、 おうちでやっておきなさい！
English: The court orders the defense to keep his practice sessions restricted to his home!

Japanese: はい‥‥。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: そのかたは‥‥？
English: Who is this...?

Japanese: アクロさんの弟です。
English: That is Acro's younger brother.

Japanese: ナニを言ってるの！ 彼はまだ、死んでないわ！
English: What does this prove? His younger brother isn't dead!!

Japanese: たしかに、 そのとおりです。
English: Technically, that's true.

Japanese: しかし、バットさんは 半年前から意識不明‥‥
English: However, Bat has been in a coma for six months now...

Japanese: アクロさんが、“殺された”と 感じたのも、ムリはありません！
English: It's not a stretch to see how Acro could feel that his brother is dead!

Japanese: ミリカさんが、彼を‥‥？
English: Regina... She did that to him...?

Japanese: バカバカしい。
English: Do you spend your entire life dreaming up new ways to be a fool!?

Japanese: 証人の弟、木下　一平のことは 当然、検察側も調べている。
English: Naturally, the prosecution has looked into Acro's brother, Sean Dingling.

Japanese: 彼は半年前、ライオンに襲われて 人事不省におちいったのよ！
English: Six months ago, he was bit by a lion and fell into his current comatose state!

Japanese: ラ‥‥ライオン‥‥ですか。
English: A l-l-lion!?

Japanese: ミリカ‥‥立見　里香は たしかに猛獣使いよ。
English: Regina... I mean, Ms. Regina Berry is an animal tamer by trade.

Japanese: でも、ライオンは、人を おそう調教など受けていない！
English: However, no tamed animal in that position is ever trained to attack another human!

Japanese: つまり、ミリカ嬢には‥‥
English: They wouldn't understand the command!

Japanese: ライオンに人間をおそわせる ことなど、できるわけがない！
English: Moreover, Ms. Regina could never do something like that! It's just not in her.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥では、 その弟さんのことは‥‥？
English: Hmm... So then what happened to Acro's brother?

Japanese: “殺人事件”ではなく、 ただの“事故”だった。
English: He's not the victim of an attempted murder, he's the victim of an accident.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: ど、どうする？　‥‥だれも ナットクしてくれないよ！
English: Now what do we do? No one seems to be going along with your theory.

Japanese: ねえ！　ホントに事故だったの？ ‥‥バットさんのこと‥‥！
English: Do you think what happened to Bat was actually an accident!?

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥ 事故‥‥だったんでしょうか‥‥
English: I suppose it was truly an accident...

Japanese: 男のコはあきらめが カンジンよ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: It's essential that a man knows when to give up... Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: やはり、ミリカさんの“殺人事件” ‥‥立証はムリ、ですか。
English: It's impossible to prove Regina's involvement in a murder attempt, isn't it...?

Japanese: ハッ！　ライオンを けしかけた、なんてね。
English: Hah! Instigating a lion to attack! Who ever heard of something like that!?

Japanese: “決定的な証拠”は コレだ、と言ってたクセに‥‥
English: And to think that the defense claimed this was "conclusive evidence"...
(...Ugghhh...)

Japanese: （‥‥“コショウ”の小ビンと ‥‥“ライオン”‥‥？）
English: (A small bottle of pepper... ...A lion... What's their connection...?)

Japanese: ああああああああああああああッ！
English: AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH!!

Japanese: どうしたの、なるほどくん！
English: What's the matter, Nick!?

Japanese: わ‥‥わかった！ やっぱり、あれは‥‥
English: I figured it out! Wow... It was right in front of my nose the entire time!

Japanese: 裁判長！　お待ちください！
English: Your Honor... Wait!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか？
English: W-What is it?

Japanese: ライオンがバットさんにかみついた のは、ただの事故ではなかった！
English: The lion biting Bat was no accident at all!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: W-WHAT!?

Japanese: コリない男ね‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: You're such an amateur, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: ミリカ嬢がライオンを けしかけるのはムリだったのよ！
English: There is no way that Regina would ever incite her lion to attack another human being!

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥
English: She may not have incited the lion to attack another human being,

Japanese: ライオンがかみついた原因は、 ミリカさんが作ったのです！
English: but Regina is responsible for making the lion bite Acro's brother, Bat!

Japanese: もう、なるほどくん！ いいの？　負けちゃっても！
English: Nick... You sure you want to go with that?
Huh?

Japanese: レオンがバットさんに かみついた証拠だよ？
English: How exactly does this evidence prove the reason Léon bit Bat?

Japanese: もっとちゃんとしたのが あるじゃない！
English: You don't think that there's something better you should present instead!?

Japanese: ‥‥もっとちゃんとしたのを 見せてください。
English: I'd like you to show me the piece of evidence please...

Japanese: （クッ‥‥！　もっと ちゃんと考えなければ！）
English: (Doh! ...I'm gonna have to think this over some more.)

Japanese: それは‥‥スカーフ‥‥ですか。
English: That's... That's just a scarf.

Japanese: アクロさん。
English: Acro.

Japanese: これは、バットさんが巻いていた ものですね？
English: This scarf is something that Bat used to wear, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。
English: That's right...

Japanese: このスカーフを、バットさんに プレゼントしたのは？
English: And who is the one that gave this scarf to Bat?

Japanese: ‥‥ミ、ミリカ‥‥です。
English: R... Regina... Regina gave it to him...

Japanese: ミリカさん‥‥
English: Regina...

Japanese: このスカーフには、血の他に、 別のものが付着しています。
English: There is something more than just blood on this scarf, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、なんですか？
English: ...And what might that be?

Japanese: コショウです。
English: Pepper.

Japanese: コショウ‥‥？
English: Pepper?

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: Pepper.

Japanese: ミリカさんは、事故の前に、 バットさんにこれをわたした！
English: Regina gave this scarf to Bat right before the accident!

Japanese: コショウを タップリふりかけて！
English: And she covered it with as much pepper as she could!

Japanese: （なな‥‥ なんだ？　このチンモクは‥‥）
English: (Hey... What's with the silent treatment?)

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥弁護人。
English: Umm... Excuse me, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ずいぶんイキオイよく 指をつきつけられましたが‥‥
English: You've done a good job of fingering a criminal...

Japanese: いったい、それが なんだと言うのですか？
English: But out of curiosity... What was her crime?
Umm...

Japanese: コショウをふりかけた スカーフをプレゼントした。
English: Regina gave a pepper covered scarf to Bat as a present.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、罪になるのですか？
English: Where's the crime in that?

Japanese: （どうやら、裁判長はまだ、 わかってないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It still seems like the judge just doesn't get it...)

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一‥‥。
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: ライオンは‥‥笑ったそうね？
English: Wasn't it said that the lion seemed to be smiling?

Japanese: わ、笑った？ ライオンが、ですか‥‥？
English: Smiling? The lion was smiling?

Japanese: バット氏が、かみつかれる 直前のこと‥‥
English: Right before Bat was bit by the lion.

Japanese: ライオンは一瞬、カオをゆがめて ‥‥“笑った”ように見えた。
English: For a moment, the lion's mouth changed and it looked like he was... Smiling.

Japanese: ライオンというのは ‥‥笑うのですか？
English: Lions... Smile?

Japanese: 聞いたことはないわ。 しかし‥‥
English: I've never heard of them smiling; however...

Japanese: クシャミはします。
English: Lions sneeze.

Japanese: な、なな、な、なな、な、ななな、
English: W-W-W-W-W-W-W-W-W...

Japanese: レオンは‥‥バットさんに かみつく気なんてなかった。
English: Léon didn't plan on biting Bat at all.

Japanese: ‥‥ただ、クシャミをした だけだったんですよ。
English: ...In reality, all he actually did was sneeze.

Japanese: スカーフにふりかけられた コショウのせいで！
English: He sneezed because of all the pepper spread on the scarf!

Japanese: なんですってェェェッ！
English: W-W-W-W-WHAT!?!?

Japanese: バカなあああァァァッ！
English: YOU FOOOOOOL!!

Japanese: ‥‥信じられません、そんな‥‥！
English: You've got to be kidding me!!

Japanese: ど、どうしましたか、狩魔検事？
English: W-What's the matter, Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: あ、あまりのコトに、 コトバを失ってしまった‥‥ I... I...
English: I object... For objection's sake...

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一！ あ、あなたはホンキでこんな‥‥
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright! You... This theory... You believe it?

Japanese: こんな冗談みたいなコトが 起こったと言うつもりなの！
English: You really plan to say this joke of an accident actually happened?

Japanese: もちろん！ ‥‥ジッサイに、起こったんです！
English: Of course I do! It's the truth.

Japanese: そして、バットさんは‥‥ 意識を断たれた‥‥。
English: The lion sneezed due to the pepper, and that's when Bat... Lost consciousness.

Japanese: こんな“冗談みたいな”事故で アクロさんは弟を失ったのです！
English: Acro nearly lost his brother due to this accident! Or this "joke" as you put it.

Japanese: だからこそ、復讐しようとした。 ‥‥ミリカさんに。
English: Which is why he tried to get his revenge... Against Regina.

Japanese: ‥‥バ、バカな‥‥！
English: You foolish idiot!
Hmm...

Japanese: ふっふっふっ‥‥ “冗談みたい”ですか‥‥。
English: So it's a "joke of an accident" to you huh?

Japanese: あなたのイマジネーションには 感服しましたよ、成歩堂さん。
English: Once again, I'm impressed by your imagination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: まさか、こんな事故をマジメに 主張してくれる人がいるとは‥‥
English: To think that there's someone who treats this accident with the respect it deserves...

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくの言ったことに、 まちがいないんですね‥‥？
English: Are you telling me that what I said was... True?

Japanese: ア、アクロさん‥‥
English: A-Acro...

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥ まさか、証人‥‥？
English: ...You don't mean!? You can't mean!? Witness...!?

Japanese: み、認めるんですか？ ‥‥弁護側の主張を‥‥！
English: A-Are you confirming the defense's claim...!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥成歩堂さん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: オレ‥‥。まだ、つかまる ワケには行かないんですよ。
English: Unfortunately, your imagination is not enough to find me guilty of murder.

Japanese: ‥‥なんですって‥‥
English: What do you mean by that?

Japanese: コショウ‥‥スカーフ‥‥ ライオン‥‥
English: The pepper... The scarf... The lion...

Japanese: ‥‥こんなバカげたハナシ、 だれが信じられますか？
English: ...I see where you're going, but it's a bit hard to swallow...

Japanese: それに‥‥。何より大きな モンダイがあります。
English: Not to mention the fact that there's an even bigger problem with your theory.

Japanese: なんですか‥‥それは？
English: What would that problem be?

Japanese: 決まってるでしょう。 証拠ですよ、証拠‥‥。
English: The same problem it's always been... Evidence.

Japanese: オレが団長めがけて マックスの胸像を投げつけた‥‥
English: If I dropped Max's bust on top of the Ringmaster...

Japanese: そんな証拠が どこにあるんですか？
English: Where is the evidence that proves that claim?
Uhhh...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ “決定的な証拠”‥‥ですか。
English: Hmm... You mean the "conclusive evidence"?

Japanese: その場合、モンダイはやはり、 “凶器”ということになるわね。
English: The biggest problem is the murder weapon, or the lack thereof to be more precise.

Japanese: きょうき‥‥
English: Murder weapon...

Japanese: 弁護人の主張する“胸像”‥‥。
English: The "bust" that the defense claims was used...

Japanese: たとえば、もしアクロ氏の 部屋から、その胸像が見つかって、
English: If that were to be found in Acro's room,

Japanese: さらに被害者の血液が付着 していれば、決定的なのですが‥‥
English: and if it was covered with the victim's blood, that would be awfully conclusive in my eyes.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかにそれは、決定的ね。
English: Yes it would be...

Japanese: （あの“胸像”か‥‥）
English: (The "bust"...)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥なんとかならないの！
English: Nick! You've gotta do something...!

Japanese: （これが、最後の１歩だ‥‥ 正しく踏み出せれば、勝てる！）
English: (This is the last step... If I get this one right, the case is won!)

Japanese: （家宅捜査を要求する というのも手だけど‥‥）
English: (It might be worthwhile to search Acro's room, but...)

Japanese: ど、どうしてそうしないの！
English: W-Why aren't you going to search his room!?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、あなたにも わかってきたようね。
English: ...It looks like you finally figured things out, didn't you?

Japanese: 狩魔家の“カンペキ主義” のイミが‥‥。
English: Now you know the true meaning of "Von Karma Total Justice"...

Japanese: まあね。
English: I guess...

Japanese: きっときみなら、それぐらいは やってるだろうと思ったんだ。
English: I figured with you that's the least I should expect. You'd leave no stone unturned.

Japanese: 弁護側は、アクロ氏の部屋の 家宅捜査を要求します！
English: The defense requests to search the room of the witness, Ken Dingling!
Hmph...
What is it now?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、あなたには まだ、わかってないようね。
English: ...It looks like you still haven't figured things out, have you?

Japanese: 狩魔家の“カンペキ主義”の イミが‥‥。
English: By now you must know the meaning of "Von Karma Total Justice"...

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥？
English: Y-You mean...?

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: A Von Karma never leaves anything to chance!

Japanese: われわれはすでにきのう、 アクロ氏の家宅捜査を行った！
English: We already searched Acro's room yesterday!

Japanese: け‥‥結果は！
English: W-What did you find!?

Japanese: 言うまでもないでしょう。
English: There's no reason to even say it.

Japanese: もしそんなモノが出てきたら、 証人として呼ぶわけがない。
English: If we found what you think we found in that room, Acro would not be here as a witness.

Japanese: この証人の部屋に、 マックスの胸像はなかった！
English: But to put a point on it, Max's bust was not in the room!

Japanese: ‥‥凶器はいまだ、行方不明よ。
English: The murder weapon is still unaccounted for.

Japanese: お聞きのとおりです。 ‥‥成歩堂さん。
English: You see, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: オレの部屋には胸像はない。
English: The bust wasn't in my room.

Japanese: 家宅捜査は、イトノコギリ刑事が 抜き打ちでおこなった。
English: Furthermore, Detective Dick Gumshoe executed the search by complete surprise.

Japanese: そしてアクロ氏には、そのまま 検事局に出頭してもらったのよ。
English: And we took Acro directly to the prosecutor's office after that.

Japanese: ‥‥以上。
English: End of story...

Japanese: （ちょ、ちょっと待ってくれ‥‥ そんなはずは‥‥）
English: (J-Just wait a second... Something's funny about all this...)

Japanese: ふっふっふっ‥‥
English: Heh heh heh heh...

Japanese: どうやら、最後のサイゴ‥‥、 ツメが甘かったみたいですね。
English: It looks like the coup d'grace in this case was ever so sweet...

Japanese: し‥‥しかし！　あのメモや スカーフは‥‥！
English: But... But... What about the scarf!? What about the note!?

Japanese: それがどうかしましたか？
English: What about them? Mr. Wright, no offense, but the important evidence here

Japanese: ダイジなのは“団長を殺害した 証拠”でしょう？
English: is about the death of the Ringmaster. I think you should remember that.

Japanese: ぐっ‥‥！
English: Arrggh!

Japanese: なんとかならないの！ せめて‥‥せめて、そうだよ！
English: Do something Nick! Don't let this case slip away!!

Japanese: 凶器が‥‥あの胸像が今、 どこにあるのか！
English: The bust... Where is it now?

Japanese: （‥‥胸像がどこにあるか‥‥？）
English: (Hmm... Where's the bust right now?)

Japanese: なるほどくんなら‥‥ わかるでしょ？　わかるよね！
English: You're Phoenix Wright! You know where that bust is! I'm sure you do!

Japanese: （手がかりはゼロ‥‥ わかるわけがない！）
English: (There's not even a single clue... How am I supposed to know where the bust is!?)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ ここまでのようですね。
English: It seems as if this case is coming to a close...

Japanese: 弁護人の反証は、今１歩、 およばなかったようです。
English: The defense's counter- arguments look to have fallen short.

Japanese: ごくろうさま‥‥。
English: Thank you for your support...
...Ack!

Japanese: 申しわけありません ‥‥成歩堂さん。
English: Now if you'll please excuse me, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: では、この証人に対する 尋問を、これにて終了します！
English: I think that brings to an end the cross-examination of this witness!

Japanese: マックスの胸像がどこにあるか‥‥
English: Where is Max's bust...?

Japanese: べ、弁護側には 立証するじゅんびがあらりまる
English: The defense needs time to prepare to present it's lace...

Japanese: ‥‥うう。キンチョーして べろ、かんじゃった‥‥。
English: I mean case. Sorry, I'm a bit nervous and I just bit my tongue.
Huh?
What?

Japanese: なんだってええッ！ ‥‥いたッ！
English: We need time to do WHAT!?!? OWWW!!

Japanese: なんであなたがイチバン おどろいてるのよ！
English: Why are you the most surprised person here!? She's YOUR aide, isn't she!?

Japanese: ほ‥‥ホントですか！ 弁護人‥‥成歩堂くん！
English: D-Do you really have a-a case to present, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: え‥‥え！　ぼくですか！
English: W-Whaaa!? Is he talking to me!?

Japanese: あとはまかせた！ なるほどくん！
English: The rest is up to you, Nick! Good luck!

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥
English: H-Hey... Wait... You can't be serious...

Japanese: アクロバットって、 命がけなんだよね？
English: Acrobat's always have their lives on the lines, don't they?

Japanese: アクロさんは今まで、 そうやって生きてきた。
English: That's how Acro's lived his life up until now.

Japanese: あたしたちも、わたるんだよ！ ‥‥この細い、ロープを！
English: Now it's time for us to walk across our own tightrope!

Japanese: そうしなきゃ、 負けちゃうんだから！
English: If we don't, we're certain to lose!

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人に うかがいましょう。
English: Very well... The defense may proceed.

Japanese: まさか‥‥わかるはずがない。
English: He doesn't have a clue... And I don't think he'll be finding one anytime soon.

Japanese: （‥‥ロジックのツナわたり‥‥ ミスはゆるされない！）
English: (...Walking the tightrope of logic... There's no room for a false step!)

Japanese: （イチかバチか‥‥ もう、進むしかない！）
English: (Sink or swim... The only way through is forward!)

Japanese: 凶器は‥‥マックスの胸像は、 今、どこにあるのッ！
English: The murder weapon... Where is Max's bust now!?

Japanese: おそらく、テント内のどこかに あるはずです！
English: It must be somewhere in the Berry Big Top!

Japanese: テ、テント内‥‥ですって！
English: I-It's... It's in the Big Top!?

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！　もっと ハッキリおねがいします！
English: Mr. Wright! Where exactly in the Big Top would we find the bust?

Japanese: たしか‥‥団長室 だったと思います。
English: To be specific, I believe it is in the Ringmaster's room.

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。思ったとおりね 成歩堂　龍一。
English: Once again... You are so predictable, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ハッタリはもう、たくさんよ。
English: Enough of your bluffing.

Japanese: 団長室はさんざん調べた。 ‥‥胸像はなかったわ。
English: The Ringmaster's room was thoroughly searched... There was no bust found.

Japanese: この狩魔　冥、命をかけて ちかってもいい！
English: There's absolutely no way I'm wrong. I'd even stake my life on it!

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。こらこら。 命をソマツにしては‥‥
English: Oh... There's no need to be staking lives on things...

Japanese: まぐうッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: そして、あなたも食らいなさい！
English: And you get one too!

Japanese: まぐうッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ダメだよなるほどくん！ ちゃんと考えないと‥‥
English: Nick! You've gotta think things over again...

Japanese: そ、そうだ‥‥きっと、 考えればわかるハズ！
English: You're right... If I think things through, I should figure it all out!

Japanese: もちろん、食堂に 決まっています！
English: Of course, it is obviously in the cafeteria!

Japanese: しょ、食堂‥‥！
English: The c-c-cafeteria...!

Japanese: いったい、なぜですか！
English: Why would it be there!?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなコトです。
English: That's easy enough...

Japanese: あそこがイチバン、 ちらかってましたからね！
English: It's the messiest place in the entire circus!

Japanese: いてェ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: バカなコトを言わないで！
English: Spare us the jokes!

Japanese: そうだよ！
English: That reminds me!

Japanese: ウチの事務所のほうが、 よっぽどちらかってるよ！
English: Our office is just as messy as that cafeteria!

Japanese: （そういうコトじゃ ないんだけど‥‥）
English: (I wouldn't go that far. It's not that messy...)

Japanese: 食堂はテッテー的に調べた！ 胸像はなかったわ！
English: We went over that cafeteria with a fine-toothed comb and found no sign of a bust!

Japanese: か‥‥考えなおします‥‥！
English: Ack! I've gotta rethink things!

Japanese: サーカスのステージに 決まっています！
English: It is on the circus' stage!

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしてですか！
English: W-What is it doing there!?

Japanese: ‥‥こんなハナシを 聞いたことがありませんか？
English: Haven't you ever heard this the saying...

Japanese: “すもうのドヒョウには、 宝物がうめられている”、と。
English: "You keep your treasure inside your castle."

Japanese: それと同じリクツです！
English: It's kinda like that!

Japanese: やっぱり考えなおします。
English: Look's like it's time to give things some more thought.

Japanese: そうしてください。
English: I agree.

Japanese: まちがいありません！ ‥‥宿舎内にあるはずです。
English: There is no doubt in my mind! The bust is somewhere around the Lodging House!

Japanese: そうなのですか！
English: You don't say!?

Japanese: そ、そんなバカな‥‥ッ！
English: He better not say...!

Japanese: さらにくわしく言うならば！
English: In fact, allow me to be even more specific as to its location! You can find it...

Japanese: もちろん、 トミーさんの部屋です！
English: Indeed, it is in Moe's room!

Japanese: ぴ、ピエロの‥‥？ まさかッ！
English: T-That clown...? You don't mean!?

Japanese: なぜ、トミーさんの部屋だと？
English: Why would the bust be in Moe's room?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなコトです。
English: That's simple...

Japanese: だってぼくは、他の部屋を 知りませんからね！
English: I couldn't think of any other rooms it could be in!

Japanese: いてェ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: バカなコトを言わないで！
English: Refrain from making yourself look stupid!

Japanese: そうだよ！
English: Nick!

Japanese: ベンさんとリロくんの立場は どうなるの！
English: What about where Ben and Trilo were standing!?

Japanese: （そういうコトじゃ ないんだけど‥‥）
English: (Hmm... Maybe she's on to something here...)

Japanese: あの部屋はテッテー的に調べた！ 胸像はなかったわ！
English: We searched that room inside out and there was absolutely no sign of a bust!

Japanese: か‥‥考えなおします‥‥！
English: L-Let... Let me try again!

Japanese: 考えるまでもない。 ‥‥アクロさんの部屋
English: Without a doubt, the bust is in... Acro's room.

Japanese: ぎゃあ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: せ、せめてサイゴまで 言わせてく
English: L-Let me finish... J-Just let me finish...

Japanese: いたたたッ！
English: OWWWWW!!

Japanese: “家宅捜査した”って 言ってるでしょう！
English: I already told you that we searched his room from top to bottom!

Japanese: 泣かすわよ！　成歩堂　龍一！
English: Prepare to taste your own tears... Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: しっかりしなさい！ 成歩堂　龍一！
English: The court orders Mr. Phoenix Wright to get a grip on himself immediately!

Japanese: ‥‥か、考えなおします。
English: I'll... Uhh... Go back and think things over.

Japanese: もちろん、広場に うめられているはずです！
English: It's buried in the plaza!

Japanese: げ‥‥現場付近ですか！ それは気がつきませんでした！
English: N-Near the scene of the crime? And no one noticed it!?

Japanese: い、いったいなぜ！ そう思うの？
English: How could you possibly think that!?

Japanese: ‥‥こんなハナシを 聞いたことがありませんか？
English: Haven't you ever heard the old saying...

Japanese: サクラの木の根もとには、 死体がうめられている、と。
English: "It was hiding right under your nose?"

Japanese: それと同じリクツです！
English: That about sums it up!

Japanese: やっぱり考えなおします。
English: I should rethink that, shouldn't I?

Japanese: そうしてください。
English: Yes, you should.

Japanese: もちろん、この法廷内です！
English: It's obvious! The bust is inside this very courtroom!

Japanese: も、もちろん‥‥？
English: It's... O-O-Obvious...?

Japanese: もっとハッキリ言うならば ズバリ、ここです！
English: Allow me to pinpoint the location of the bust once and for all!
It's... Umm...

Japanese: なな、なんですか！ 急にダマりこんで‥‥
English: W-W-W-What!? Why you did you stop talking all of a sudden!?

Japanese: い、いや‥‥はたして、 これを言っていいものかと‥‥
English: Well... I'm just not really sure if I should say anything or not because the bust is...

Japanese: 裁判長席ですッ！
English: Under Your Honor's bench!

Japanese: ‥‥あなた、言っていいコトと 悪いコトがあ
English: ...You had to think about whether it was a good idea to say that!?

Japanese: くおぉらあああぁぁァァッ！
English: GWWWWAAAAHHHH!!

Japanese: この私に、なんということを！ ペナルティですッ！
English: WHAT DID YOU JUST SAY TO ME!? THAT'S A PENALTY!!

Japanese: うわッ！
English: Arrrgghhh!

Japanese: もういっちょ！ ダブルで食らいなさいッ！
English: WHAT'S THAT!? YOU WANT A DOUBLE!? HERE YA GO!!

Japanese: ぐわあああァァッ！
English: Double arrrggghhh!!

Japanese: （うう‥‥イヤな予感が したんだよな‥‥）
English: (Ahh... I knew I had a bad feeling about that...)

Japanese: さっさとやりなおしなさいッ！
English: Now hurry up and fix things!
It's... Umm...

Japanese: なな、なんですか！ 急にダマりこんで‥‥
English: W-W-W-What!? Why you did you stop talking all of a sudden!?

Japanese: い、いや‥‥はたして、 これを言っていいものかと‥‥
English: Well... I'm just not really sure if I should say anything or not because the bust is...

Japanese: 検事席ですッ！
English: Under the prosecutor's bench!

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥そんな。 ‥‥狩魔検事がッ！
English: No... You don't mean that... MS. VON KARMA!

Japanese: ぐあああッ
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ぎいいいッ
English: OWWWWWW!!

Japanese: ぐうううッ
English: OWWWWWWWWW!!

Japanese: ぐえええッ
English: OWWWWWWWWWWWW!!

Japanese: ぐおおおおおッ！
English: OWWWWWWWWWWWWWWW!!

Japanese: そしてモチロン、 キサマにはこれよッ！
English: Don't think you're getting off either!

Japanese: なな、なるほどくん！ 立ってよお！　なるほどくうん！
English: N-Nick! Get up, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥つづけて。裁判長。
English: ...Proceed Your Honor.

Japanese: と、とりあえず弁護人が 目をさましませんとねえ‥‥
English: I will when Mr. Wright opens his eyes...

Japanese: だいじょうぶ？ なるほどくん‥‥
English: Nick... Are you OK?
W-W-Who is this girl?

Japanese: （なんて、キオクを 失ってる場合じゃない！）
English: (This is no time for me to be losing my memory!!)

Japanese: ‥‥アクロさん。
English: Acro...

Japanese: すみませんが、その車イスの ヒザかけを取ってください。
English: I'm sorry to ask this, but do you mind if I take the blanket off your wheelchair?

Japanese: よく聞こえませんでしたね、 成歩堂さん。
English: I'm sorry, I didn't quite hear you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、体が大きい。 当然、車イスだって‥‥大きい。
English: Well... You are a big guy... And you have a pretty big wheelchair because of it...

Japanese: そのヒザかけの下になら、 ちょっとしたものをかくせる。
English: I just wanted to make sure you weren't hiding anything under that blanket.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。胸像の１個ぐらい、 カンタンに、ね‥‥。
English: Because it seems to me that it'd be really easy to say... Hide a bust under there...

Japanese: ‥‥ふっふっふっ‥‥
English: ...Hah hah hah hah hah...

Japanese: 冗談がお好きなようですね ‥‥成歩堂さん。
English: Once again, your penchant for humor hits me where it hurts, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: この状況で笑えるのは、 たいしたものだと思います。
English: I think it's pretty amazing that you could laugh in your position...

Japanese: ‥‥でも‥‥。 胸像は、そこにあるはずなんです。
English: However, your lightheartedness doesn't change the fact that the bust is under there.

Japanese: あなたは、１人では 宿舎から出ることができない。
English: We all know that you couldn't leave the lodging house by yourself in your condition.

Japanese: そんな状況で‥‥きのう、 狩魔検事が捜査令状を持ってきた。
English: That proved inconvenient when Ms. von Karma happened to search your room yesterday.

Japanese: 凶器の像を発見されてしまっては、 すべてがおしまいです。
English: If she had found the murder weapon in your room, it would have been all over.

Japanese: とっさに、それをかくせる 場所は‥‥１つしかなかった。
English: Which is why you had to hide it. In the only place that you could hide it...

Japanese: ‥‥その車イスの、中です。
English: Under your wheelchair.

Japanese: アクロさん。もう一度、言います。
English: Which is why, Acro, I have to ask you again.

Japanese: ヒザかけを取ってください。
English: Could you please remove the blanket from the wheelchair?

Japanese: おみごとです。
English: Well done, Mr. Wright. Masterfully played.

Japanese: ‥‥そんな‥‥バカなッ！
English: ...You!! You fool!

Japanese: こんなハズは‥‥
English: How could you...!?

Japanese: オレの負けです。 さすが、プロですね。
English: You've got me. I've been bagged by a real pro.

Japanese: ‥‥おふたりとも。
English: Actually, two of them...
(Two of them...?)

Japanese: 狩魔検事。
English: Ms. Franziska von Karma and Mr. Phoenix Wright.
...What!?

Japanese: １つうかがいたいのですが‥‥。
English: There's just one thing I'd like to know...

Japanese: どうして、きのうになって急に 家宅捜査なんか、したんです？
English: How did you know to launch the surprise search on my room last night?

Japanese: 決定的な証拠は、２つ。 マントと、マックスの胸像‥‥
English: There were two pieces of decisive evidence... The cloak and the bust...

Japanese: マントは、部屋で燃やして ゴミといっしょに捨てました。
English: I burned the cloak in my room and threw the ashes away with the trash.

Japanese: ‥‥ゴミは毎朝、ミリカが 持ち出してくれるんですよ。
English: ...Regina always took my trash out every morning, you know.

Japanese: でも‥‥胸像は そういうワケには行かない。
English: But the bust... Obviously I couldn't throw that away.

Japanese: 突然の家宅捜査‥‥。オレは、 それをかくすしかなかった。
English: When you executed your search, all I could do was try and hide the bust.

Japanese: そして、かくす場所は‥‥ この、車イスの下しかなかった。
English: And the only place that I could hide it quickly was under this wheelchair...

Japanese: ‥‥そこまで、読んでいたんじゃ ないんですか？
English: Ms. von Karma, you had things all figured out, didn't you?

Japanese: オレは、あなたがたの クールな作戦にハマってしまった。
English: I was completely sucked in by your calculated strategy...

Japanese: 法廷の真ん中で‥‥凶器を かくしてるのを見つかっちゃあ、
English: And now to be caught in the middle of court hiding the murder weapon...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、言いワケしようが ないですからね。
English: ...There's no way I can escape that.

Japanese: 負けましたよ、 成歩堂さん‥‥狩魔検事さん。
English: So you've got me. Well done, Mr. Wright. Well done, Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥ やはり、さすがですなあ‥‥。
English: Hmm... It all makes sense now.

Japanese: か、かるま検事‥‥ そこまで考えていたんだ！
English: I can't believe that Von Karma thought that far ahead... It's amazing!

Japanese: うーん、あのカオを 見るかぎり‥‥
English: Uh huh... You definitely couldn't tell by looking at her.

Japanese: そうは思えないんだけど‥‥
English: I know I sure couldn't.

Japanese: ‥‥あり得ない‥‥ この私が‥‥しくじるなんて！ I...
English: ...I can't believe it... Failed!

Japanese: なぜ私は、家宅捜査なんかした？ あんなことさえしなければ‥‥
English: Why did I order a surprise search of your room...? If only I hadn't done that...

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、私たちは 真相にたどりついたようですね。
English: It doesn't matter now though. It seems as if we've arrived at the truth.

Japanese: アクロさん。
English: Acro.

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: あなたは‥‥立見　七百人を 殺害しましたか？
English: Did you kill the Ringmaster of the Berry Big Circus, Mr. Russell Berry?

Japanese: はい。オレがやりました。
English: Yes, Your Honor. I'm responsible for that crime.

Japanese: アクロさん‥‥
English: Acro...

Japanese: 弟は‥‥ミリカの気を引こうとして ‥‥よくイタズラをしていました。
English: All my brother did... was want Regina to like him. That's why he'd tease her.

Japanese: ある日、弟はミリカに コショウをふりかけたんです。
English: One day, my brother sprinkled some pepper on Regina.

Japanese: ミリカは、ひどいクシャミをして ‥‥みんなで笑いました。
English: She started sneezing so hard... You couldn't help yourself from laughing.

Japanese: それで、ミリカは弟に仕返しを‥‥
English: That's why Regina thought it'd be funny to get him back in the same way...

Japanese: それが‥‥ あのスカーフだったんですか。
English: And that's why she covered the scarf in pepper...

Japanese: 彼女には、悪気はなかった。 ‥‥それはわかっていました。
English: I know she didn't want anything bad to happen... I know this...

Japanese: ただ単純に、弟にクシャミを させたかっただけ‥‥。
English: She just wanted to make my brother sneeze a few times too...

Japanese: でも‥‥オレには、それが ユルせなかった！　どうしても。
English: But... I just couldn't forgive her! No matter what.

Japanese: 自分が何をしでかしたのか？ ‥‥彼女には、永久にわからない！
English: What am I truly guilty of? ...I'm guilty of never, ever being able to understand her.

Japanese: “弟が星になった”‥‥ そんなバカげたことを信じて、
English: "Your brother became a star." Regina believed in that so purely,

Japanese: ムジャキに‥‥あまりにも ムジャキに笑っている！
English: that she would laugh innocently when saying it... Too innocently...

Japanese: どうしても‥‥ ガマンできなかったんです。
English: I just couldn't stand it... No matter how hard I tried.

Japanese: それで‥‥ミリカさんを‥‥
English: That's when you decided to do something about Regina...

Japanese: なんて‥‥コト‥‥
English: What do you mean by that...?

Japanese: あるイミ‥‥あなたも被害者 だった、ということですか。
English: So are you saying that you are a victim in all of this as well?
No... That's not what I mean.

Japanese: ただの殺人犯人ですよ。 ‥‥オレは。
English: I'm nothing but a murderer. That's who I am...

Japanese: 最初は、自殺を考えました。 次に、自首をしようかと‥‥。
English: At first, I thought I'd kill myself. Then I pondered giving myself up...

Japanese: ‥‥でも。オレはまだ、 いなくなるワケにはいかなかった。
English: But... I couldn't just up and leave... I just couldn't...

Japanese: だから‥‥マ、マックスに‥‥ オレの‥‥罪を‥‥！
English: That's why... I tried... to pin this... on Max.

Japanese: マックス‥‥すまない‥‥！
English: Max... I'm so sorry...!

Japanese: ‥‥オレは‥‥ まだ、いなくなるわけには‥‥
English: I just... I just... I just couldn't up and leave yet...

Japanese: ‥‥フシギな事件でした。
English: This has been such a strange case...

Japanese: まるで‥‥そう、 サーカスを見ているような。
English: It's almost a reflection of the circus itself.

Japanese: ‥‥バカ‥‥な‥‥ この私が‥‥
English: I'm... An... Idiot...

Japanese: キサマごときに、 二度までも‥‥
English: I can't believe it... You... Beat me... Again.

Japanese: とにかく、今度こそ ギモンの余地はありません。
English: I believe this case is now beyond any point of possible discussion.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人に 判決を言いわたしましょう。
English: Thus, I'd like to declare my verdict.

Japanese: ‥‥では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: This court is adjourned.

Japanese: 同日　午後　４時２７分 地方裁判所　被告人第５控え室
English: December 30, 4:27 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 5

Japanese: んゴオオォォージャスゴージャス！
English: F-F-F-FABULOUS!!

Japanese: ‥‥って、スナオに よろこべないな。
English: But to be honest, I can't really be too happy about this.

Japanese: アクロさんも、団長さんも、 ミリカちゃんも、バットさんも‥‥
English: Acro, the Ringmaster, Regina, and Bat...

Japanese: 悪いヒト、 いなかったんですよね‥‥？
English: Not a single one of them was a bad person inside, huh?

Japanese: どうなんだろう。 ボクにはわからないよ。
English: That's a good question. And one I don't know the answer to.

Japanese: おめっとーさん！ おめっとさんミスタ・マックス！
English: Congratulations! Congratulations Mr. Max!

Japanese: あ‥‥ありがとう。
English: T-Thank you.

Japanese: もお！　どーしたの、 このシメっぽいフンイキは！
English: What's with the vibe in this room?

Japanese: アクロさんのコト考えると‥‥
English: We're just thinking about Acro...

Japanese: ダーメダメ！
English: No no no no no!

Japanese: ヒトのこと気にしてたら、一生 スナオになんかよろこべないって！
English: If you worry about people too much, then you'll be like this forever, and never be happy!

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Huh?

Japanese: うわああああああああああああん！
English: WAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHHH!!

Japanese: ‥‥ミリカもさっきから、 このとおりだよ。
English: ...She's been like this for a while now.

Japanese: うわああああああああああああん！ ‥‥ミリカのせいなんだあ！
English: WAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHHH!!! IT'S ALL MY FAULTTTTTT!! WAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHHH!!

Japanese: ス‥‥スウィート・ハニイ‥‥
English: S-S-Sweetie... Sweetie pie...

Japanese: バットも、アクロも‥‥ もう帰ってこないんだあ！
English: Bat and Acro... They're never coming back!!

Japanese: このまま‥‥みんな、 ばらばらになっちゃうんだあ！
English: Now... Now everyone's gonna split up!!

Japanese: ミリカちゃん‥‥
English: Regina...

Japanese: 弁護士さん！　ねえ、教えて！
English: Mr. Wright! Tell me something!

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんだい。
English: W-What do you want to know, Regina?

Japanese: さっきアクロ、言ってたよね！
English: Acro said something right at the end...

Japanese: ‥‥オレはまだ、いなくなる わけにはいかないんですよ‥‥
English: ...I just couldn't up and leave yet...

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱりアクロ、 まだミリカのコト‥‥
English: ...Does that mean that Acro...

Japanese: ミリカにフクシュウしようと しているんでしょ！
English: Is he gonna try and get his revenge on me!?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。 でも、それはしかたないよ。
English: I think that's his plan... But what can you do about it?

Japanese: ミリカちゃんは、それだけのことを してしまったんだから。
English: You did do some awfully bad things to him, Regina.

Japanese: う‥‥うわあああああああああん！
English: Uwwaaahh... WAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHH!!

Japanese: や、やっぱり‥‥そうなんだあ！ もう、ユルしてもらえないんだあ！
English: Y-You're right...! He won't ever forgive me will he!?

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥。追い打ちを かけちゃったぞ‥‥）
English: (Yikes! It looks like I struck the final blow on Regina...)

Japanese: ミリカちゃん‥‥カワイソウ‥‥。
English: Poor Regina...

Japanese: そ、そんなことはないよ。
English: He's not gonna do that to you, Regina.

Japanese: ‥‥ホント？　ホントにそうなの！ ミリカ‥‥信じてもいいの？
English: Are you sure? You're really sure!? I can believe that...?

Japanese: うん。‥‥アクロさんはもう、 復讐なんて考えてないと思うよ。
English: Yep. Acro doesn't have any desire for revenge anymore...

Japanese: それなら、ミリカにも！ ミリカにもショーコを見せて！
English: If that's true... Then I want to see some evidence!!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: アクロがあんなコトを言った、 本心を教えてよう！
English: Acro said that all the time to you, so I thought it'd work for me too...

Japanese: どう‥‥かな。 ナットクしてくれた？
English: So what about this? Now do you understand there's no need to worry?
Uwaaahhh...

Japanese: うわああああああああああああん！ ヤッパリ、ウソだったんだあ！
English: WAAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHHH!! You were lying to me!!

Japanese: みんな‥‥ミリカにホントのコト なんて、教えてくれないんだあ！
English: No one ever tells me the truth!! No one ever tells me what's going on!!

Japanese: （しまった‥‥ まちがえたらしいな‥‥）
English: (Doh! Looks like I made a mistake with that one...)

Japanese: ミリカちゃん‥‥カワイソウ‥‥。
English: Poor Regina...

Japanese: アクロさんは、なんとしても つかまりたくなかったんだよ。
English: Acro didn't want to get caught for a reason.

Japanese: ‥‥弟さんが目をさますのを 待つために。
English: He wanted to see his brother open his eyes again...
B-Bat...!

Japanese: そうだね。バットさん‥‥ まだ、生きてるんだから！
English: That's right, Regina... He's still alive you know.

Japanese: そうだったんだ‥‥
English: He is, huh...?

Japanese: でも‥‥、 つかまっちゃったね。アクロ。
English: But now that Acro's been caught...

Japanese: ‥‥そうだよね。
English: Uh huh...

Japanese: わかった！
English: I know!
What...?

Japanese: ミリカが。
English: I'll do it!

Japanese: ‥‥ミリカがかわりに、 ずっとバットのそばにいる！
English: I'll stay next to Bat as long as it takes!

Japanese: バットが目をさまして‥‥ アクロに会える日まで！
English: Until he opens his eyes... And then until he can meet Acro again!

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。 それがいいと思うよ。
English: That's so sweet of you, Regina.

Japanese: ごめんね‥‥バット‥‥ アクロ‥‥。
English: I'm sorry Acro... I'm sorry Bat...

Japanese: （これでよかったのかな‥‥）
English: (Well, hopefully this is enough to give her a little peace of mind...)

Japanese: なあ、マックス。
English: Hey, Max.

Japanese: なんだい？
English: What is it, Moe?

Japanese: アンタにはホント、 メイワクかけちまったな。
English: We really put you through a lot, didn't we buddy? I'm sorry about what happened.

Japanese: いいぜ、いつ出ていっても。 契約は解消する。カネもはらうよ。
English: So whenever you'd like to leave us, I'll pay your fee and rip up the contract.

Japanese: ゴージャス！　了解した。 明日にでも出ていこう。
English: I understand! Such a fabulous thing to do for me... I might even leave tomorrow.

Japanese: これから、このサーカスは どうなるんだい？
English: What's going to happen to the circus now?

Japanese: ああ、それな。
English: Ahh... That's the big question.

Japanese: ‥‥オレたちの団長の チカラはホント、スゴいよ。
English: Our Ringmaster was really an amazing person, wasn't he?

Japanese: いなくなっても、まだみんなを ガッチリまとめちまってる。
English: Even though he's not here anymore, everyone is sticking together.

Japanese: 芸人もスタッフも、みんな‥‥ 出ていきたくない、って言うんだ。
English: The staff, the performers... No one wants to leave the circus.

Japanese: それでな‥‥
English: That's why I've made a decision...

Japanese: それで？
English: What is it?

Japanese: つづけることにしたよ。 オレっちが団長になって、さ。
English: I've decided that I will take over as the new Ringmaster.

Japanese: アンタに負けない、ゴージャスな ‥‥世界一のサーカスを目ざす！
English: I'll turn this circus into the best circus this world has ever seen!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥世界一だって？
English: The best circus the world has ever seen!?

Japanese: あ、ああ。‥‥笑うなよな！
English: D-D-Don't laugh!

Japanese: それ、すごいですね。
English: Amazing...

Japanese: わあ！　楽しみだなあ！
English: Yay! I can't wait!!

Japanese: それなら‥‥ ハナシはちがってくるな。
English: Then I guess that changes things...

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 世界一のサーカスには、 ゼッタイ必要なものが１つ、ある。
English: There's only one thing the best circus the world has ever seen needs.

Japanese: 世界一のイリュージョン‥‥
English: The world's best illusions...

Japanese: そう、大魔術が！
English: Which means this circus needs the best magician the world has ever seen!

Japanese: ‥‥マックス‥‥
English: Max...

Japanese: ゴージャスに行こうじゃないか！ ‥‥団長クン！
English: Let's work together and make our circus SUPER FABULOUS! Whaddya say big guy?

Japanese: あ‥‥ありがとう。
English: I don't know what to say... All I can say is thank you.

Japanese: ホラ。ミリカちゃんも。
English: Umm... Regina, you're gonna help them out too, aren't you?

Japanese: ミ、ミリカはもう‥‥いいよぉ。
English: Umm... I don't know. Maybe the circus would be better of without me.

Japanese: ナニ言ってんだよ、ミリカ。
English: What are you talking about, Regina?

Japanese: 今日、なんのために 連れてきたと思ってるんだい？
English: Why do you think that I brought you to court today?
Uhh...

Japanese: みんなで、生まれ変わるんだよ。 タチミ・サーカスは！
English: We've got to work together to make the Berry Big Circus bigger than it's ever been!

Japanese: ト、トミーさん‥‥
English: M-Moe...

Japanese: そういうコトだ！ スウィート・ハニイ！
English: Moe's right, sweetie pie! It can't be the Berry Big Circus without Regina Berry!

Japanese: ‥‥マックス‥‥。
English: M-Max...

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥なんとか、うまく おさまったみたいだね‥‥
English: It seems like everything is going to turn out alright here...

Japanese: ゼッタイ、見に行くよ！ 世界一のサーカス！
English: I can't wait to go see the best circus the world has ever seen!

Japanese: 最高にゴージャスな席を用意して、 お待ちしているよ。
English: We'll save you the most fabulous seats!

Japanese: ‥‥ま。 チョイと時間はかかるケド。
English: It'll take us a while to get ready, but I'm going to order special whoopee cushion seats!

Japanese: あひゃ！　あひゃひゃあひゃ。 ひゃらひゃらあひゃらひゃら。
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: ‥‥そうか‥‥ 決め手は、やはり‥‥？
English: I see... What made the case?

Japanese: ご指示のとおりだったッス。 きのうの家宅捜査が効いたッス！
English: Just like you thought... Yesterday's search really paid off, sir!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。見抜いていたッスか？ きのうの時点で‥‥真相を。
English: Umm... You had it all figured out yesterday, didn't you?

Japanese: まさか‥‥
English: It was just a theory...

Japanese: ただ、もし彼が犯人ならば、 結末はこれしかないと思った。
English: If Acro really was the killer, I thought this was the only way it could end.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士があの男なら、な。
English: Especially if "he" was the defense attorney...

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一‥‥ッスか。
English: You mean Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥さて。そろそろ 飛行機が出る時間だ。
English: Of course... Well Detective, my plane is about to leave.

Japanese: アクロ氏のことなら、 悪いようにはしない。
English: Do me a favor and try not to be too harsh on Acro.

Japanese: ‥‥私がそっちへ行って、 検事局長とかけあうつもりだ。
English: Once I get back, I'll make a stop by the Chief Prosecutor's office.

Japanese: ハッ！
English: Yes, sir!

Japanese: では、お待ちしているッス！ ‥‥御剣検事どのッ！
English: I'll be waiting for you! Goodbye Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ■第三話　逆転サーカス おわり
English: Episode 3: Turnabout Big Top THE END

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、山田　耕平に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Billy Bob Johns...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
